title: dodecatheon pauciflorum f. album
intro: Dodecatheon pauciflorum f. album, also known as the western trout lily, is a flowering plant species in the gentian family, characterized by its unique white and pink flowers. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid and glycoside content. The western trout lily has been used to treat various health issues, such as skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to promote healing and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and salves, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat a range of health concerns.
